title: Some BATS cleanup: run and systemd tests
body:
run test: run positive test before negative; and actually
implement real negative tests. Note that '--conmon-pidfile'
will happily clobber an existing file; I don't know if that
is desired behavior. Also, add confirmation tests for
cidfile/pidfile, not just 'exit status is good'.

systemd test: enable rootless, and again add actual content
testing.

Signed-off-by: Ed Santiago <santiago@redhat.com>

comments.body: LGTM but want a signoff from @vrothberg before merge

[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *edsantiago*, *mheon* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Flibpod). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/master/OWNERS)~~ [mheon] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
I'd rather not merge with that giant FIXME. @vrothberg can you comment on whether or not --conmon-pidfile should clobber existing file?

> I'd rather not merge with that giant FIXME. @vrothberg can you comment on whether or not --conmon-pidfile should clobber existing file?

cidfile and conmon-pidfile behave differently. AFAICS, it's expected that cidfile fails (Docker compat) while pidfile overwrites (to make conmon simpler).
Thank you. I've removed the FIXME and the commented-out code. Tests are green.
